content: This document provides structured guidance for architectural design decisions during creative phases, ensuring comprehensive evaluation of options and clear documentation of architectural choices.
In an Architecture Decision Record, the context must be documented, including system requirements and technical constraints.
In an Architecture Decision Record, component analysis must be documented, including core components and their interactions.
In an Architecture Decision Record, architecture options must be documented, including a description, pros, cons, technical fit, complexity, and scalability for each.
In an Architecture Decision Record, the final decision must be documented, including the chosen option, rationale, and implementation considerations.
In an Architecture Decision Record, the validation must be documented, including how requirements were met, technical feasibility, and a risk assessment.
Architecture should be evaluated based on criteria including Scalability, Maintainability, Performance, Security, Cost, and Time to Market.
The architecture design must be verified to ensure all system requirements are addressed.
The architecture design must be verified to ensure component responsibilities are defined and interfaces are specified.
The architecture design must be verified to ensure data flows are documented.
The architecture design must be verified to ensure security considerations are addressed.
The architecture design must be verified to ensure scalability and performance requirements are met.
The architecture design must be verified to ensure the maintenance approach is defined.
For implementation readiness, it must be verified that all components are identified and their dependencies are mapped.
For implementation readiness, it must be verified that technical constraints are documented.
For implementation readiness, it must be verified that a risk assessment has been completed.
For implementation readiness, it must be verified that resource requirements and timeline estimates are defined.
